purpose: The cos-lending-selling-datatools repository is a data processing platform for the Cross River Bank lending and selling business, designed to extract, transform, and load data from source systems into data warehouses. It uses Airflow DAGs to orchestrate DBT transformations and data ingestion tasks from MSSQL databases, providing analytical capabilities for loan contracts, seasoning, and volume data.

apis.purpose: Executes DBT or ingestion tasks in ECS Fargate

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Core loan data entity representing lending transactions

dataEntities.name: LoanType
dataEntities.description: Categories of loans processed in the system

dataEntities.name: Contract
dataEntities.description: Legal agreements governing loan terms and conditions

dataEntities.name: Fee
dataEntities.description: Fee structures and calculations for loans

dataEntities.name: TermSheet
dataEntities.description: Document outlining the terms of loan agreements

dataEntities.name: Seasoning
dataEntities.description: Loan aging information used for HFS and LTHFS calculations

messagingPatterns.name: ECS Task Queue
messagingPatterns.description: Manages execution of DBT and ingestion tasks in ECS Fargate

findings.title: Hardcoded S3 credentials
findings.description: Local S3 credentials are hardcoded in aws_utils.py. Although these appear to be for local development only, hardcoded credentials pose a security risk if accidentally used in production contexts. Replace with environment variables or configuration files that are excluded from source control.

findings.title: Incomplete ingestion task implementation
findings.description: The bootstrapping_dag.py contains TODO comments indicating incomplete implementation of ingestion tasks. The ingestion task factory is referenced but the local implementation is not completed ('not_implemented' function in ingestion_task_factory.py), which would prevent proper execution in local development environments.
